mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive or neutral 60-day forward returns in over 50% of historical instances where both series decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 220, an increase in real savings deposits signals defensive household behavior that reduces equity demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between high-CPI/low-RSAFS and other regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no predictive power for 20-day equity returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls fall below 130 million while the unemployment rate remains above 4.0%, it confirms a structural labor market deterioration that triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined threshold fails to generate negative forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain stress periods where industrial production growth exceeds 2.0% month-over-month, elevated capacity utilization compresses equity valuations as inflation expectations rise, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in over 60% of triggered instances, indicating that supply growth is priced as a positive earnings driver rather than an inflation risk.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with declining real savings deposits indicates tightening financial conditions that restrict corporate liquidity and equity risk appetite, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS changes are orthogonal to RSAFS trends, or forward returns show no significant spread between high-tightening and baseline regimes.
